strengths: 18+ years of full-stack experience — triple the 6-year bar — with sustained TypeScript, Node.js, React, and MongoDB product work at Optilyz, RIDE Capital, and re:cinq
Current hands-on AI agent development at re:cinq, building MCP code generators and agentic-workflow frameworks — a direct match for the JD's AI agent requirement
Deep testing and craftsmanship credentials: TDD, pairing, CI/CD, trunk-based development in production teams, plus authored test frameworks and taught multi-day testing courses
concerns: No explicit Nest.js or Vitest experience listed, though extensive adjacent Node.js and testing-framework depth suggests a fast ramp
Kotlin is absent and Java exposure appears dated (2016–2018 robotics work), leaving the secondary stack thinner than the primary
Recent frontend history leans on Ember.js; dedicated React experience is concentrated in the Optilyz role

key_factors.factor: Full-stack TypeScript/Node.js depth well beyond required experience
Active AI agent and MCP tooling development
Testing, TDD, and engineering-practice leadership matching code-quality responsibilities

response.justification: This candidate is a strong fit for the Senior Fullstack Developer role: he brings roughly three times the required experience across frontend and backend, with recent, directly relevant TypeScript/Node.js/React product work including pricing systems at scale on AWS with MongoDB. His current role building AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling squarely satisfies the JD's rarest requirement, AI agent development. He demonstrates polyglot proficiency (TypeScript, Java, D, C++, PHP, Swift), database breadth (MongoDB, MySQL, SQL), Docker/Kubernetes deployment experience, and a proven craftsmanship culture — TDD, CI/CD, code katas, conference talks, and teaching — that maps directly onto the role's emphasis on code quality, reviews, mentorship-level collaboration, and process improvement. Gaps in Nest.js, Vitest, and Kotlin are framework-level rather than conceptual, and his record of rapidly mastering new stacks (from Apple TV apps to low-latency microservices handling millions of requests per second) strongly suggests he would close them quickly.
